visuals.title: Lecture Note: Single Pole and a Zero
visuals.caption: The note derives the magnitude and phase expressions for H(s)=(s+C)/(s+B) with 0<C<B.

visuals.title: Magnitude with One Zero and One Pole
visuals.description: For C=1 and B=2, the magnitude rises from 0.5 toward 1 as frequency increases.

visuals.title: Positive Phase Hump
visuals.description: The zero lifts the phase above 0 degrees before the phase returns toward 0 degrees at high frequency.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: Phase Peak
visuals.caption: The note remarks that the phase increases from 0 degrees, then decreases after omega grows beyond the pole region.

visuals.title: Lecture Note: H(s)=(s+1)/(s+2)
visuals.caption: The example sketches a rising magnitude and a positive phase hump, then comments that the behavior resembles a high-pass filter.
